jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams] - Homer's classic exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes or gets hurt in opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation followed by screaming
jokes.explanation: Homer's catchphrase is perfectly integrated to his character and iconic, but not original at this point in the series

jokes.text: Aw, how come bears can crap in the woods and I can't?
jokes.setup: Homer tells Bart to use a plastic bag for his business in nature
jokes.punchline: Bart questions the double standard between humans and bears
jokes.explanation: Clever observation with perfect Bart voice - questioning adult hypocrisy with childlike logic

jokes.text: Like a raisin covered in chocolate. Or a monkey in a cowboy suit.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining how the racetrack surrounding the bird sanctuary is wonderful
jokes.punchline: His bizarre analogies, especially the monkey in cowboy suit
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer absurdist logic with wonderfully random imagery that's pure character

jokes.text: Show us your boobs. Show us your boobs. [Homer] You better do what he says, Marge.
jokes.setup: A parrot has learned to mimic racetrack crowd behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately supports the parrot's demand
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer opportunism - using any excuse to get what he wants while maintaining plausible deniability

jokes.text: Why do jerks think everyone wants to see their stupid name?
jokes.setup: Someone has carved their name in a tree
jokes.punchline: Lisa's mature observation about juvenile behavior
jokes.explanation: Standard Lisa wisdom - competent but not particularly original

jokes.text: You're on fire! Yeah, I have won a lot of races.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out that race car driver Clay Babcock is literally on fire
jokes.punchline: He misinterprets it as praise for his racing success
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection joke with literal vs. figurative interpretation

jokes.text: Oh, sure, if you want to drive the 'traditional' way.
jokes.setup: Bart asks if he should keep his hands on the wheel while driving
jokes.punchline: The professional driver dismisses basic safety as merely 'traditional'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how experts can dismiss basic safety as outdated thinking

jokes.text: Hi-diddly-ho, pedal-to-the-metal-o-philes.
jokes.setup: Ned arrives at the racetrack
jokes.punchline: His signature greeting modified with racing terminology
jokes.explanation: Pure Ned Flanders - his wholesome greeting style applied to an unlikely context

jokes.text: I like the fresh air and looking at the poor people in the infield.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining what he likes about racing beyond the safety gear
jokes.punchline: His casual elitist observation about enjoying watching poor people
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly subversive - reveals unexpected class snobbery beneath Ned's Christian niceness

jokes.text: Now, honey, they's my parents too.
jokes.setup: Someone complaining about Cletus parking by their parents
jokes.punchline: Cletus reveals he's married to his sister/cousin
jokes.explanation: Expected hillbilly incest joke for Cletus character but well-executed reveal

jokes.text: Abso-not-ly, hot Roddy.
jokes.setup: Rod asks if they can move closer to the action
jokes.punchline: Ned's portmanteau of 'absolutely not' with racing pun on Rod's name
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's perfectly in Ned's voice - wholesome dad puns with racing theme

jokes.text: Move your damn butt. Bite me.
jokes.setup: Ned says they're out of range of drivers' cursing
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by drivers cursing
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing comedy - immediate contradiction of Ned's claim with economy of setup

jokes.text: Be patient, Son. A watched car never crashes.
jokes.setup: Bart is impatient waiting for a crash
jokes.punchline: Homer's twist on 'a watched pot never boils'
jokes.explanation: Solid parody of common saying applied to morbid racing context

jokes.text: Oh, I missed one!
jokes.setup: Homer's advice about watched cars, then a crash happens
jokes.punchline: Bart's disappointment at missing the crash proves Homer wrong
jokes.explanation: Immediate payoff to previous joke - proves Homer's wisdom wrong with perfect timing

jokes.text: Hmm. A Ford urinating on a Chevrolet.
jokes.setup: Ned examines a crude t-shirt design
jokes.punchline: His clinical, innocent description of the offensive imagery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned voice - describing crude imagery in the most innocent, clinical way possible

jokes.text: Don't you usually laugh at everything? Yes. Yes, I do.
jokes.setup: Ned doesn't laugh at the crude t-shirt
jokes.punchline: Homer's meta-observation about Ned's character trait, which Ned confirms
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about established character traits with Ned's self-aware confirmation

jokes.text: Mommy has bosoms like that. Yeah, I wish.
jokes.setup: Child innocently comments on cheerleader's anatomy
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate agreement and longing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer inappropriateness but relies on basic sexual humor

jokes.text: I'll get some hot dogs. No foot-longs. I know. They make you uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Normal offer to get hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Maude's specific restriction and Ned's understanding acceptance
jokes.explanation: Innocent marital quirk that becomes tragic irony later - sets up major emotional beat

jokes.text: That's a damn lie, and you know it! Gimme a shirt!
jokes.setup: T-shirt girl says no one else wants a shirt
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-the-top aggressive response to obviously false claim
jokes.explanation: Homer's disproportionate outrage over a minor slight - perfect character escalation

jokes.text: Ooh, a bobby pin!
jokes.setup: Chaos as t-shirts are being fired, people screaming
jokes.punchline: Homer finds delight in a completely mundane discovery amid the chaos
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - Homer's childlike focus on trivial discovery during tragedy sets up dark irony

jokes.text: This is eating up a lot of time. Maybe just a pat on the butt. Yeah, that works.
jokes.setup: Marge's heartfelt speech about telling loved ones how special they are
jokes.punchline: Homer's practical solution to emotional expression
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer practicality undermining Marge's emotional moment with efficient affection

jokes.text: Aye. Yarr. Oh, glavin! Why, glavin?
jokes.setup: Reverend talking about people without memorable catchphrases
jokes.punchline: Characters immediately demonstrate their catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing - characters proving the opposite of the eulogy's point about memorable phrases

jokes.text: Yeah. Lots of storybooks have witches. Shut up, Kirk!
jokes.setup: Reverend calls the Van Houtens' marriage a storybook romance
jokes.punchline: Kirk's bitter correction about fairy tale antagonists
jokes.explanation: Darkly comic subversion of romantic cliché with bitter marital reality

jokes.text: Finally.
jokes.setup: 21 t-shirt gun salute for Maude's funeral
jokes.punchline: Bart's satisfied reaction to getting the t-shirts he wanted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Bart's materialistic satisfaction at a funeral callback

jokes.text: The boys at headquarters will not like it, but I'm getting pretty sick of them and their Bombay attitude!
jokes.setup: Apu giving Maude's Squishy points to Ned
jokes.punchline: His rebellion against corporate headquarters and cultural specificity
jokes.explanation: Good Apu character moment - his immigrant experience and corporate frustration

jokes.text: Oh, I really mean it though. I mean, if it was you that died, I would've been on her so fast!
jokes.setup: Moe trying to comfort Ned about Maude being special
jokes.punchline: His inappropriate confession about wanting to pursue Maude
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character - trying to be nice but revealing his inappropriate thoughts

jokes.text: Send me to Maude. That's it. Here I come, baby. Oh, yeah!
jokes.setup: Ned attacking Moe for the inappropriate comment
jokes.punchline: His dramatic plea to join Maude in death with orgasmic-sounding delivery
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable comedy from Ned's grief manifesting as inappropriate sexual-sounding death wish

jokes.text: Is it love? Kindness? Ooh, tough room.
jokes.setup: Bart asking what cheers up the Flanders boys when they're feeling low
jokes.punchline: Their innocent suggestions followed by Bart's comedian-like response
jokes.explanation: Great character contrast - innocent Flanders boys vs. Bart's worldly comedian sensibility

jokes.text: No, you just winged him and made him a Unitarian.
jokes.setup: Playing Billy Graham's Bible Blaster video game
jokes.punchline: Rod's explanation of partial religious conversion as game mechanic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant religious satire - treating denomination as damage level in video game logic

jokes.text: But the sprinkler is gone. It's time to let go. I just bought that.
jokes.setup: Homer giving grief counseling metaphor about letting go
jokes.punchline: Ned's literal concern about his actual stolen sprinkler
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - Homer's metaphor about grief vs. Ned's literal property concern

jokes.text: I can't believe my last words to Maude were, 'No foot-longs.'
jokes.setup: Ned reflecting on his final conversation with Maude
jokes.punchline: The mundane, restrictive nature of his last words to her
jokes.explanation: Poignant callback that transforms earlier innocent joke into tragic irony

jokes.text: I'm the one who parked in the ambulance zone, preventing any possible resuscitation.
jokes.setup: Homer listing ways he contributed to Maude's death
jokes.punchline: The final, most damning revelation about blocking medical help
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation peak - Homer's casual admission of the most serious contribution to the tragedy

jokes.text: Do you even have a job anymore? I think it's pretty obvious that I don't!
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning Homer's constant availability
jokes.punchline: Homer's cheerful admission of unemployment
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about Homer's inexplicable free time and casual attitude toward unemployment

jokes.text: Dad, there are other wipes besides star wipes. / Why eat hamburger when you can have steak?
jokes.setup: Lisa criticizing Homer's overuse of star wipes in video editing
jokes.punchline: Homer's food analogy defending star wipes
jokes.explanation: Good Homer confidence in his amateur video editing choices

jokes.text: Why eat hamburger when you can have steak?
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting other video transitions besides star wipes
jokes.punchline: Homer's passionate defense of star wipes using food metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obsession with unnecessary but showy film techniques treated as gourmet preference

jokes.text: Why eat hamburger when you can have steak?
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting other wipes besides star wipes
jokes.punchline: Homer's passionate defense of star wipes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obsession - his arbitrary strong opinions are always funny

jokes.text: Single women of Springfield, your prayers have been 'Flanswered.'
jokes.setup: Dating video for Ned begins
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of Flanders + answered
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly captures Homer's approach to dating video marketing

jokes.text: So that's why you maced me.
jokes.setup: Video shows Ned crying after being approached
jokes.punchline: Homer's realization about why Ned defended himself
jokes.explanation: Good callback revealing hidden context for earlier interaction between Homer and Ned

jokes.text: Oh, I would date Ned in a second if I was a woman or gay. He looks like a cuddler, that Ned. I-I like that. I like to be held. I like to be pampered.
jokes.setup: Homer giving testimonial for Ned's dating video
jokes.punchline: Homer reveals too much about his own intimate desires
jokes.explanation: Homer's TMI moment - awkward oversharing in attempt to help

jokes.text: I like to be held. I like to be pampered.
jokes.setup: Homer giving testimonial for Ned's dating video
jokes.punchline: His oversharing about his own emotional needs instead of praising Ned
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer self-centeredness - making Ned's dating video about his own needs

jokes.text: I'm just a 'Q' without a 'U'.
jokes.setup: Ned feeling lonely and incomplete after Maude's death
jokes.punchline: Scrabble metaphor for being incomplete without his partner
jokes.explanation: Beautiful metaphor that's perfectly in character - wholesome game reference expressing genuine loneliness

jokes.text: John Wayne, Tupac Shakur, Sherlock Holmes. Sherlock Holmes is a character. He sure is.
jokes.setup: Homer listing eligible bachelors in heaven for Maude to date
jokes.punchline: Ned's correction about Holmes being fictional, Homer's confident agreement
jokes.explanation: Homer's confident ignorance - mixing real and fictional people then doubling down when corrected

jokes.text: Damn, I've just been indicted. Looks like we're gonna have to reschedule. How's your July? Mine's terrible. Let's talk in August.
jokes.setup: Professional woman bragging about her success and sexuality
jokes.punchline: Sudden criminal indictment and casual rescheduling around legal troubles
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from professional bragging to criminal activity treated casually

jokes.text: 'Cause him's a commitment dog, isn't him?
jokes.setup: Woman talking to her dog during video dating message
jokes.punchline: Baby talk to dog about relationship commitment
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe comedy about overly attached pet owner using baby talk

jokes.text: I'm about as exciting as a baked potato. You're darn right you are! And you've got lots of other great qualities too!
jokes.setup: Ned's self-deprecating comparison to boring food
jokes.punchline: Homer's enthusiastic agreement instead of reassurance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - enthusiastically confirming the insult instead of offering comfort

jokes.text: Well, I've had it!
jokes.setup: Ned's complaints about church chocolate quality to God
jokes.punchline: His declaration of being fed up with the Almighty over confectionery
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Ned's breaking point is over chocolate quality, not wife's death

jokes.text: No, I'm not kiddin'! I am gonna sit right here and miss church. You just watch.
jokes.setup: Rod and Todd's shocked reaction to Ned skipping church
jokes.punchline: Ned's defiant declaration of his mild rebellion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Ned's idea of extreme rebellion is sitting in a chair

jokes.text: Oh, my gosh. Some dropouts must have overpowered the reverend.
jokes.setup: Electric guitars playing in church
jokes.punchline: Ned's assumption that criminals have taken over rather than church innovation
jokes.explanation: Good Ned character moment - his sheltered worldview making him assume the worst

jokes.text: This is a love song about a dude I met in a sleazy motel. - A dude named God. - Oh.
jokes.setup: Christian rock singer talking about meeting a dude in a sleazy motel
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's about God, congregation's relief
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - the setup sounds scandalous before the reveal

jokes.text: So? She's good-lookin'. Shut up. You shut up.
jokes.setup: Marge pointing out the Christian rock singer is talking about the Bible
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive focus on appearance and marital bickering
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer shallowness and marital dynamics but economically delivered

jokes.text: So when your soul has gone astray / Just let God be your triple A / He'll tow you to salvation / And he'll overhaul your heart
jokes.setup: Christian rock song about salvation
jokes.punchline: Extended automobile metaphor for religious salvation
jokes.explanation: Clever automotive metaphor for Christian rock - well-executed parody

jokes.text: I toileth not on ye Sabbath, woman! A pox on thee!
jokes.setup: Marge asking Homer to help Rachel with her equipment
jokes.punchline: Homer's fake biblical language to avoid Sunday work
jokes.explanation: Homer's opportunistic fake religiosity with comically archaic language to avoid helping

jokes.text: And I'm here every week, rain or shine.
jokes.setup: Ned giving Rachel his information for when she returns
jokes.punchline: His reliable church attendance as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback showing Ned's return to character after brief rebellion
